mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5 percent within a single month, labor market deterioration triggers recession fears that compress equity risk premiums and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses 4.5%, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a sharp drop in consumer credit balances signals a synchronized demand shock that reduces corporate revenue expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns when both INDPRO and RSAFS decline simultaneously, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a statistically insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid policy tightening, rising short-term rates increase borrowing costs for highly leveraged firms, compressing profit margins and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral during sustained FEDFUNDS hikes when CPIAUCSL is below 3.0% annualized, or the mechanism shows no directional consistency across multiple tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP indicates expanding economic activity that boosts corporate earnings expectations, mechanically supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth fails to correlate with positive 20-day equity returns, or the rolling Spearman rank correlation drops below 0.10 over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index falls below 98.0, supply chain bottlenecks or capacity constraints trigger cost-push inflation that erodes corporate margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns do not decline when INDPRO drops below 98.0, or the signal produces false breakouts in more than 50% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate signals a tightening labor market that accelerates wage inflation, prompting the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy and suppressing equity valuations over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS and UNRATE signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns when both indicators show labor market tightness, or the interaction effect disappears in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: In a housing market downturn, declining housing starts reduce construction activity and consumer wealth effects, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as economic growth expectations fall.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive despite sustained declines in HOUST, or the historical relationship reverses direction during periods of low mortgage rates.
